cross_registry_links.note: agonia (G0074) appears in both registry 002 (agony) and registry 005 (anguish) word sets.
Multiple chul sub-entries (H2342A/H/I/J/K) appear in both registries, sharing the same CHUL root.
tsa.rah (H6869B) is a primary distress term that appears in the anguish word set; likely also in the distress registry.
ya.gon (H3015) glossed as grief, sorrow, anguish — likely shared with the sorrow registry.
mar (H4751) glossed bitter/bitterness — appears in anguish word set but primary semantic home may be bitterness registry.

data_quality_flags.description: Three terms in the STEP header list (lines 30, 36, 44) have no corresponding word analysis block in the body of the file: tsu.qah (H6695 or similar), me.tsu.qah (H4691), and hardship (stenochōria G4728). They appear only in the related-word clusters of mu.tsaq and ma.tsoq. No verse data exists for these three terms. They cannot be given term_inventory entries.

ra.ah (H7451C) has 684 stated occurrences and 151 verses listed in the source file. The primary meaning is evil, misery, distress, injury — this is the general Hebrew word for evil and harm. Its inner-being qualification rests on the distress/misery sense (1a) only, which is a small part of its usage. As a word-level analysis tool for anguish specifically, this term's verse set overwhelmingly addresses external harm and divine judgment rather than inner states of anguish.

enemy/tsar (H6862C) has 69 occurrences and its primary meaning is adversary, foe, enemy, oppressor. This is the relational/military sense of tsar, not its inner-being distress sense. Most of its 65 listed verses describe military or relational adversaries. Its qualification as an inner-being anguish term is not established by its own meaning block.

ir (H5892A) means excitement, anguish of terror. One of its three listed verses (Jos 21:37) is a city list with no inner-being content; the Hos 11:9 verse ('I will not execute my burning anger') does not use anguish language. Only Jer 15:8 ('I have made anguish and terror fall upon them') actually uses the word in an anguish context. This is a thin and unreliable inner-being term.

be firm/chil (H2342B) has a meaning of 'to be firm, strong' — not an inner-being state. It appears in this word set through root proximity to the CHUL family. Its two listed verses (Job 20:21; Psa 10:5) do not use anguish language. This term does not qualify as an inner-being anguish term.

sunochē (G4928) has 2 occurrences. The meaning block cites Lk 21:25 and 2Cor 2:4, but only Lk 21:25 appears in the verse list in this file. 2Cor 2:4 ('out of much affliction and anguish of heart') is listed under thlipsis (G2347) in this file, not under sunochē. The verse set for sunochē is therefore incomplete as given in this source file.

chil (H2427A) is listed here with only 3 verses (Exo 15:14; Psa 48:6; Mic 4:9), whereas the same term in registry 002 (agony) is listed with 6 verses (adding Jer 6:24; Jer 22:23; Jer 50:43). The anguish file provides a subset of the agony file's verse set for this term. This is an inconsistency between files rather than an error — the full set is in registry 002.

chal.cha.lah (H2479) appears in the file header's initial Hebrew terms list and in the STEP term list. The body verse set here shows Eze 30:4, Eze 30:9, Nah 2:10 — but is missing Isa 21:3, which appeared in the registry 002 agony file. The anguish file's verse set for this term is incomplete compared to registry 002.

Three section headers are missing the '###' markdown heading marker: (1) 'anguish (odunē)' at line 152 has '###anguish' with no space; (2) 'to twist: tremble (chul)' at line 1415 has no '###' prefix; (3) 'travail (ponos)' at line 1571 has no '###' prefix. These format errors cause section boundary failures in automated parsing.

a.mal (H5999) has 55 occurrences and its primary meaning is toil, trouble, labour. The anguish connection is through the misery/trouble sense. Only a small subset of the 54 listed verses directly address an inner state of anguish; the majority address external labour, toil, or mischief. The most significant inner-being verse is Isa 53:11 ('out of the anguish of his soul').
